body:
### Problem

Bumped into test failure when upgrading the Alpine Linux neovim package from 0.11.x to 0.12.1.

This smells like a real bug and not like a broken test.

```
FAILED   1 test, listed below:
FAILED   test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua @ 1022: :terminal buffer does not drop data when job exits immediately after output #3030
test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua:1004: Row 1 did not match.
Expected:
  |*19995: TEST{MATCH: +}|
  |*19996: TEST{MATCH: +}|
  |*19997: TEST{MATCH: +}|
  |*19998: TEST{MATCH: +}|
  |*19999: TEST{MATCH: +}|
  |^[Process exited 0]                                |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
Actual:
  |*18854: TEST                                       |
  |*18855: TEST                                       |
  |*18856: TEST                                       |
  |*18857: TEST                                       |
  |*18858: TEST                                       |
  |^[Process exited 0]                                |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |

To print the expect() call that would assert the current screen state, use
screen:snapshot_util(). In case of non-deterministic failures, use
screen:redraw_debug() to show all intermediate screen states.

Snapshot:
screen:expect([[
  18854: TEST                                       |
  18855: TEST                                       |
  18856: TEST                                       |
  18857: TEST                                       |
  18858: TEST                                       |
  ^[Process exited 0]                                |
  {5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
]])

stack traceback:
        test/functional/ui/screen.lua:914: in function '_wait'
        test/functional/ui/screen.lua:537: in function 'expect'
        test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua:1004: in function 'check_term_rep'
        test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua:1023: in function <test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua:1022>


 70 SKIPPED TESTS
 1 FAILED TEST
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
$NVIM_LOG_FILE: /home/ncopa/aports/community/neovim/src/neovim-0.12.1/build/nvim.log
(last 10 lines)
WRN 2026-04-09T09:20:29.014 ?.54135    vim_mktempdir:3322: $TMPDIR tempdir not a directory (or does not exist): "/home/ncopa/aports/community/neovim/src/neovim-0.12.1/build/Xtest_tmpdir"
WRN 2026-04-09T09:20:29.497 ?.54147    vim_mktempdir:3322: $TMPDIR tempdir not a directory (or does not exist): "/home/ncopa/aports/community/neovim/src/neovim-0.12.1/build/Xtest_tmpdir"
WRN 2026-04-09T09:20:29.506 ?.54149    vim_mktempdir:3322: $TMPDIR tempdir not a directory (or does not exist): "/home/ncopa/aports/community/neovim/src/neovim-0.12.1/build/Xtest_tmpdir"
WRN 2026-04-09T09:20:29.520 ?.54151    vim_mktempdir:3322: $TMPDIR tempdir not a directory (or does not exist): "/home/ncopa/aports/community/neovim/src/neovim-0.12.1/build/Xtest_tmpdir"
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
E5113: Lua chunk: 
```

### Steps to reproduce

On Alpine Linux system, build with:

```sh
        cmake -B build -G Ninja \                                                                   
                -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=MinSizeRel \                                                     
                -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr \                                                       
                -DCMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR=lib \                                                        
                -DENABLE_LTO=TRUE \                                                                 
                -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILE=TRUE \                                                     
                -DENABLE_TRANSLATIONS=ON                                                       
        cmake --build build                        
```

Then run the tests:
```sh
TEST_FILE=test/functional/terminal/buffer_spec.lua cmake --build build --target functionaltest
```


### Expected behavior

All tests pass.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12.1

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

n/a

### Operating system/version

Alpine Linux edge

### Terminal name/version

xfce-terminal, ssh, tmux

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

not installed. happens when building alpine linux package

comment_data.body: This may be a Linux kernel bug. Please provide the kernel version.
Also, according to your build flags, you are building with bundled libuv 1.52.1, right?

> This may be a Linux kernel bug. Please provide the kernel version.
```
$ uname -a
Linux ncopa-edge-x86-64 6.18.21-0-lts #1-Alpine SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC 2026-04-03 11:21:05 x86_64 Linux
```
I was wondering if it could be due to expectations on stdio flushing which are not met with musl libc, which usually strictly follows POSIX and not GNU libc expectations.

> Also, according to your build flags, you are building with bundled libuv 1.52.1, right?

It looks like I build with system libuv.
```console
$ grep LIBUV build/CMakeCache.txt 
LIBUV_INCLUDE_DIR:PATH=/usr/include
LIBUV_LIBRARY:FILEPATH=/usr/lib/libuv.so
//ADVANCED property for variable: LIBUV_INCLUDE_DIR
LIBUV_INCLUDE_DIR-ADVANCED:INTERNAL=1
//ADVANCED property for variable: LIBUV_LIBRARY
LIBUV_LIBRARY-ADVANCED:INTERNAL=1
$ readelf -d build/bin/nvim 

Dynamic section at offset 0x36f020 contains 35 entries:
  Tag        Type                         Name/Value
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libluv.so.1]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [/usr/lib/lua/5.1/lpeg.so]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libtree-sitter.so.0.26]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libutf8proc.so.3]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libintl.so.8]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libunibilium.so.4]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libluajit-5.1.so.2]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libuv.so.1]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libc.musl-x86_64.so.1]
 0x000000000000001d (RUNPATH)            Library runpath: [/usr/lib/lua/5.1:]
...
$ apk version libuv
Installed:                                Available:
libuv-1.51.0-r0                         = 1.51.0-r0 
```

I will try upgrade libuv 1.52.1 and build with bundled libuv and report back.

Thank you for your help so far!
The test failing with libuv 1.51.0 is expected. At least libuv 1.52.0 is needed for it to pass.

I can confirm that tests passes with libuv 1.52.1.

Thank you very much for you help, and sorry for the noise.

title: test failure :terminal buffer does not drop data when job exits immediately after output #3030
